query.sentence: Identify PFAS free thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing with a 2:1 shrink ratio that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing that has an application / service temperature of up to ~200°C and a shrink temperature under 250°C.

properties: Thermal stability: service temperature up to ~200 °C
Mechanical strength: sufficient tensile and puncture resistance for tubing application
Chemical resistance: compatible with common solvents, oils, and electrical insulation requirements
Non‑PFAS composition: no fluorinated monomers or additives
Processability: melt extrusion capability at lab scale
Dimensional stability: low shrinkage prior to activation
constraints: Shrink temperature must be below 250 °C to avoid damage to typical substrates
Material must be available in grades suitable for extrusion into tubing
Cost and safety considerations for lab‑scale production
No use of PFAS or perfluoroalkyl substances

final_candidate.material_name: Polyether Ether Ketone (PEEK) – Grade PEEK‑HT (e.g., 3M™ PEEK‑HT)
final_candidate.justification: PEEK is a non‑fluorinated, high‑performance polymer with a service temperature up to 260 °C and excellent mechanical properties. Commercially available heat‑shrink tubing made from PEEK (e.g., 3M™ PEEK‑HT Shrink Tubing) offers a 2:1 shrink ratio and activates at ~240–250 °C, meeting the required shrink temperature constraint. The material is melt extrudable, PFAS‑free, and has proven chemical resistance for electrical insulation applications.

process_recipe.description: Dry PEEK pellets to remove moisture before extrusion.
process_recipe.conditions: 120 °C, 4 h in a convection oven or vacuum dryer.

process_recipe.description: Melt extrude PEEK into tube geometry using a single‑screw extruder with a heated barrel set to 350–360 °C.
process_recipe.conditions: Barrel temperature 350–360 °C, screw speed 30–50 rpm, die pressure ~5–10 bar.

process_recipe.description: Cool the extruded tube in a controlled water bath to set dimensions and prevent warping.
process_recipe.conditions: Water temperature 20–25 °C, flow rate sufficient to bring tube surface to <80 °C within 30 s.

process_recipe.description: Apply a shrink coating by co‑extrusion of a thermoplastic elastomer (e.g., TPU or polyolefin) that shrinks at 200–250 °C, or use a pre‑formed PEEK heat‑shrink film laminated onto the tube.
process_recipe.conditions: Co‑extruder barrel temperature 260–280 °C for the coating layer; overall wall thickness ~0.5 mm (PEEK) + 0.1 mm (coating).

process_recipe.description: Cut the tube to desired lengths and perform a post‑heat treatment at 240–250 °C for 2 min to activate shrink properties.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature 240–250 °C, dwell time 2 min in an oven or hot air chamber.
